skill.name: low-frequency-thread-monitor
skill.description: Protocol for ETA-aware monitoring of background Codex worker, review, and planning threads without over-polling or interrupting active work.
skill.summary: Monitor background Codex threads with low-context checks until a terminal state appears.
skill.author: ggsq003-png

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and medium patterns, but review found a documentation-only skill with no executable scripts. Markdown backticks, install examples, external links, and the ~/.codex install path explain the detections; no prompt injection or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code and fenced examples. The README includes user-run install commands, but the skill itself does not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown formatting or documentation examples, not executable Ruby backticks. No script or runtime file invokes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: External Links Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The network detections are hardcoded URLs in badges, related-work links, and clone instructions. They do not show runtime network calls or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in README content and installation instructions. No evidence found of automated network access from the skill.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory Access Is an Install Path
low_findings.description: The filesystem detections point to ~/.codex/skills in clone instructions. This is a normal user-selected install location, not hidden file access by code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path is shown inside README installation commands only. No evidence found that the skill reads or writes hidden files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain descriptive monitoring text, related-work wording, or YAML display metadata. They do not mention weak algorithms or system reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation and no reconnaissance command at these locations. The matches appear to be substring-based scanner noise.

content.value_statement: Controller windows can close early, poll too often, or interrupt active background work. This skill provides a simple monitoring protocol with ETA-aware checks and terminal-state handling.
actual_capabilities: Guides low-frequency monitoring of active Codex worker threads.
Defines terminal states such as final report, failure, blocker, handoff, or Owner-input request.
Recommends next check timing based on task size, elapsed time, and near-finish signals.
Limits context use by reading recent status before terminal state.
Separates worker self-report from review, approval, absorption, and routing decisions.
limitations: Does not launch workers, manage tmux sessions, or run background processes.
Does not provide automatic timers unless the host environment supports them.
Does not authorize writes, approvals, gate unblocks, or routing changes.
Requires the user or controller to provide access to the target thread status.

use_cases.title: Keep a controller window active
use_cases.description: Monitor a background Codex worker until it reports completion, failure, blocker, or a need for Owner input.

use_cases.title: Reduce context use during review
use_cases.description: Check only recent worker status during long reviews, then read final artifacts after a terminal state appears.

use_cases.title: Coordinate multi-window workflows
use_cases.description: Separate worker self-report, independent review, absorption, approval, and next routing decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Start a quiet monitor
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin monitoring an existing worker thread.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use low-frequency-thread-monitor to watch this background worker. Keep monitoring active until a final report, blocker, failure, handoff, or Owner-input request appears.

prompt_templates.title: Schedule the next check
prompt_templates.scenario: The worker is still running and a new check time is needed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use low-frequency-thread-monitor to choose the next check time from the latest status, elapsed time, task size, and estimated remaining work.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a near-finish signal
prompt_templates.scenario: The worker says it is finalizing or preparing a report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use low-frequency-thread-monitor for a near-finish worker. Avoid interrupting it, read only recent status, and schedule the next check within the near-finish range.

prompt_templates.title: Absorb a terminal report
prompt_templates.scenario: The worker has completed, failed, blocked, or requested Owner input.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use low-frequency-thread-monitor to stop polling, read the final report carefully, verify named artifacts if needed, and recommend the next allowed action.

output_examples.input: The worker is still running and last reported that it is reviewing files.
output_examples.output: Monitoring is still active. The worker has not reached a terminal state. Next check should be in about four to seven minutes.

output_examples.input: The worker says it is writing the final summary.
output_examples.output: This is a near-finish signal. Do not interrupt the worker. Schedule the next read in about one to three minutes.

output_examples.input: The worker posted a final report with artifact paths.
output_examples.output: Stop polling. Read the final report, verify the named artifacts if needed, then recommend the next allowed action.

best_practices: Read only recent status while the worker is still running.
Use shorter intervals when the worker is finalizing or writing a final report.
Verify final artifacts before recommending approval, absorption, or routing.
anti_patterns: Do not send routine nudges to a worker that is still active.
Do not treat a pending next check as completed monitoring.
Do not merge worker self-report with review, approval, or routing authority.

faq.question: Does this skill run background workers?
faq.answer: No. It is a monitoring protocol for existing Codex worker, review, or planning threads.

faq.question: Can it replace Goal mode?
faq.answer: No. It complements Goal mode for ordinary controller windows and lighter multi-window workflows.

faq.question: When should polling stop?
faq.answer: Polling stops when a final report, failure, blocker, handoff, or Owner-input request is visible.

faq.question: Does it read full transcripts?
faq.answer: Not by default. It recommends reading only recent status until a terminal state appears.

faq.question: Can it approve completed work?
faq.answer: No. It helps verify the status and recommends the next allowed action without granting approval itself.

faq.question: Why use ETA-aware checks?
faq.answer: ETA-aware checks reduce wasted waiting time while avoiding excessive polling and context growth.
